summary: Fire‑filled Help>Slip>Franklin’s and soaring Broken Arrow
blurb: The show shines with a legendary Help → Slip → Franklin’s sequence and an epic Jack Straw jam. Jerry delivers ballistic solos while Phil's vocals soar on Broken Arrow. The second set maintains high energy through Days Between and a beautiful drum/space segment.
review: August 22, 1993 at Autzen Stadium captured one of the Dead’s most exhilarating late‑summer outings. From the opening “Jack Straw” – which even survived Bob’s brief walk‑off due to guitar gremlins – the band built momentum, but it was the second set that truly lit the night. A scorching Help → Slip → Franklin’s Tower runs like a comet across the sky, with Garcia’s Lightning Bolt guitar (some call it “acoustic‑ish bite,” others note a thin edge) delivering one of his most ballistic solos of the era. Phil Lesh steps into the spotlight on “Broken Arrow,” his voice flawless and full‑throated, while Vince Welnick, though occasionally described as “lost but keeping chords,” provides just enough synth texture to let the jams breathe. The rhythm section is unstoppable: Mickey’s drumming is called “electric,” Bill locks in tight, and their drum/space excursion glides into a soaring “Days Between.” Highlights like the extended Jack Straw jam, “The Music Never Stopped,” and “Corinna” round out an evening that many fans rank among ’93’s finest. With crystal‑clear soundboard fidelity and a mix that captures both precision and raw energy, this performance stands as a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead in top form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, with reviewers repeatedly praising the Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence, Garcia's scorching solos, and overall high‑energy performance. No consistent negative comments appear, just occasional minor mix notes, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with superb balance and no dropouts, widely praised as the definitive source.

key_highlights: Legendary Help → Slip → Franklin’s sequence that dazzles from start to finish
Epic Jack Straw jam despite Bob's brief stage exit
Soaring Broken Arrow with Phil's flawless vocals
Beautiful drum/space segment and high‑energy Days Between

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers ballistic, scorching solos throughout, especially on the Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequence; his Lightning Bolt guitar draws mixed comments but overall fuels the show’s fire.
band_performance.Phil: Phil shines vocally on Broken Arrow, hailed as flawless, and embraces the 'Let Phil Sing' banner despite a fleeting note of strain.
band_performance.Bob: Bob exits briefly during Jack Straw due to guitar woes, adding drama; when back he provides solid rhythm chops, though some reviewers note frustration in that moment.
band_performance.Vince: Vince’s keyboard work is described as 'lost but keeping chords' with lower‑mix synth tones, yet his playing supports the jams without detracting from the overall glow.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming is called electric; his contributions to the drum/space segment are highlighted as a high point of the performance.
band_performance.Billy: Bill delivers tight, energetic playing, powering the drum‑solo sections and keeping the rhythmic drive relentless.
